title: Deliciously Crispy British Pork Sunday Roast
description: A classic British dish featuring crispy pork, apple juice, and Mediterranean-friendly flavors, perfect for a Sunday roast. This recipe is sure to energize your taste buds with its mustardy and crispy texture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Score the pork skin in a crisscross pattern, cutting about 1/4 inch deep.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, Dijon mustard, and honey.
Rub the mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to get some under the skin as well.
Season the pork with salt and pepper, then place it in a roasting pan.
Roast the pork in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, then reduce the heat to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Continue roasting the pork for an additional 20 minutes per pound, or until the skin is crispy and golden brown.
While the pork is roasting, cook the lardons in a pan over medium heat until crispy, then set aside.
After the pork has finished roasting, let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving with the crispy lardons and a drizzle of apple juice.
Garnish with rosemary sprigs and serve immediately.
